messages.content: hi
Hello! How can I assist you today?
how to index through chars in string in rust
code to get 5 last chars in string in rust
code to get 5th to 8th chars from string in rust
thread 'main' panicked at 'byte index 6 is not a char boundary; it is inside 'è' (bytes 5..7)
thread ‘main’ panicked at 'byte index 6 is not a char boundary; it is inside ‘è’ (bytes 5…7)

shortest code in rust to transform string of non-ASCII characters into a) array named array_of_chars b) something that can be threat like some_name[7..9]  to obtain from 7th to 9th non-ASCII characters

two shortest possible  code snippets in rust to transform string of non-ASCII characters into a) array named array_of_chars b) something that can be threat like some_name[7..9]  to obtain from 7th to 9th non-ASCII characters

Certainly! Here are two of the shortest possible code snippets in Rust to achieve the transformation you described:

a) Transforming the string into an array named `array_of_chars`:
```rust
let string = "Hèllö, Wörld!";
let array_of_chars: Vec<char> = string.chars().collect();
```

In this code snippet, `chars()` retrieves an iterator over the characters of the string, and `collect()` converts that iterator into a `Vec<char>`, representing an array of characters. The resulting array is assigned to the variable `array_of_chars`.

b) Obtaining a slice from the 7th to 9th non-ASCII characters:
```rust
let string = "Hèllö, Wörld!";
let non_ascii_slice = &string.chars().filter(|c| c.is_ascii()).skip(7).take(2).collect::<String>();
```

In this code snippet, `chars()` retrieves an iterator over the characters of the string. The `filter()` method is used to ensure that only non-ASCII characters are considered. `skip(7)` skips the first 7 characters, and `take(2)` takes the next 2 characters. Finally, `collect::<String>()` collects the filtered characters into a new `String`, which represents the desired slice.

Note: These code snippets assume that non-ASCII characters are any characters that are not within the ASCII range (0-127).

Please let me know if you need further clarification or assistance!

two shortest possible code snippets in rust to transform string of non-ASCII characters into a) array named array_of_chars b) something that can be threat like some_name[7…9] to obtain from 7th to 9th characters while avoiding issues with byte indexing causing by non-ASCII characters

Certainly! Here are two shortest code snippets in Rust to transform a string containing non-ASCII characters into an array of chars (`array_of_chars`) and extract a substring (`some_name[7..9]`) without issues related to byte indexing caused by the non-ASCII characters:

a) Transforming the string into an array named `array_of_chars`:
```rust
let string = "Hèllö, Wörld!";
let array_of_chars: Vec<char> = string.chars().collect();
```

In this code snippet, `chars()` is used to obtain an iterator over the characters of the string. The iterator is then collected into a `Vec<char>`, representing an array of characters. The resulting array is assigned to the variable `array_of_chars`.

b) Extracting a substring from the 7th to the 9th characters using `some_name[7..9]`:
```rust
let string = "Hèllö, Wörld!";
let some_name: String = string.chars().skip(7).take(2).collect();
```

This code snippet uses `chars()` to iterate over the characters of the string. `skip(7)` skips the first 7 characters, and `take(2)` takes the next 2 characters. The filtered characters are then collected into a `String` called `some_name`, representing the desired substring.

By using the `chars()` method, these code snippets correctly handle non-ASCII characters and avoid issues related to byte indexing.
